title: Enhancing strength and ductility in Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C austenitic low-density steels through Ni and Cr alloying synergy adding
abstract_summary: A Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C lightweight austenitic steel is developed by strategic Ni and Cr alloying combined with ultra-fast cooling during thermo-mechanical processing. The study investigates how Ni and Cr modulate nanoscale κ-carbide precipitation thresholds and deformation substructures, achieving yield strength >1100 MPa, ultimate tensile strength >1200 MPa, and total elongation >45%. The excellent strength-ductility synergy is attributed to grain boundary reinforcement, solid solution hardening, precipitation strengthening from nanoscale precipitates, and activation of secondary slip system dislocations, with Cr promoting high-density dislocation arrays and refining dynamic slip bands to enhance strain hardenability.
research_objective: To develop a Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C austenitic low-density steel with density below 6.8 g/cm³ that overcomes the strength-ductility trade-off through synergistic Ni and Cr alloying and ultra-fast cooling, and to elucidate the corresponding strengthening and deformation mechanisms.
material_systems: Fe–20Mn–9Al–1.5C (wt%) austenitic low-density steel alloyed with Ni and Cr
long-range ordered (LRO) domains with L1₂ structure
main_process_variables: alloying concentrations of Ni and Cr
tensile deformation to various cumulative strains

key_sections.heading: 1. Introduction
key_sections.role: Literature review on Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels, role of κ-carbides, effect of Ni and Cr alloying, and motivation for the study.

key_sections.heading: 2. Materials and experimental details
key_sections.role: Description of steel preparation, thermo-mechanical processing (ultra-fast cooling), and characterization techniques (EBSD, TEM, computational analysis).

key_sections.heading: 3.1 Microstructure
key_sections.role: Characterization of initial microstructure prior to deformation, including grain structure, precipitates, and ordered phases.

key_sections.heading: 3.2 Tensile response
key_sections.role: Room-temperature tensile properties (yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, total elongation) and comparison with other low-density austenitic steels.

key_sections.heading: 3.3 Microstructural evolution upon deformation
key_sections.role: Evolution of deformation substructures (dislocation arrays, slip bands) as a function of strain, observed via TEM/EBSD.

key_sections.heading: 4.1 Formation of an E2₁-type ordered structure promoted by Cr or Ni alloying
key_sections.role: Analysis of how Ni and Cr additions influence the precipitation and ordering of nanoscale κ-carbides and LRO domains.

key_sections.heading: 4.2 The contribution increasement in yield strength after Ni–Cr alloying
key_sections.role: Quantitative discussion of strengthening mechanisms (grain boundary, solid solution, precipitation, and dislocation strengthening).

key_sections.heading: 4.3 Evolution of deformed substructures during the strain process
key_sections.role: Discussion of strain hardening behavior, high-density dislocation array (HDDA) formation, dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR), and their impact on ductility.

key_sections.heading: 5. Conclusion
key_sections.role: Summary of key findings on strength–ductility synergy and design guidelines for Ni-Cr alloyed austenitic lightweight steels.
